title: Grammy Winners 2026: Bad Bunny, Kendrick Lamar and Immigration Criticism
text: Bad Bunny's historic Album of the Year win at the 2026 Grammy Awards took place amid broader themes of immigration and social justice. Bad Bunny used multiple stage appearances to advocate for immigrant rights, declaring We're not savage, we're not animals, we're not aliens. We are humans and we are Americans. The artist's passionate advocacy resonated with the audience and set the tone for the evening's messaging. Immigration emerged as a dominant theme throughout the ceremony, with multiple winners using their acceptance moments to criticize immigration enforcement. Bad Bunny's Album of the Year victory for Debí Tirar Más Fotos became the first time a Spanish-language album claimed the top prize in that category. Kendrick Lamar emerged as the night's biggest winner with five Grammy Awards, including Record of the Year with SZA for Luther. Bad Bunny followed with three wins total, including Album of the Year. The achievement marked a watershed moment for Latin music representation at the Grammy Awards, coinciding with increased voting membership from the Latin Recording Academy.
